message:
fix(stdlib): guard abort() against re-entrant invocation

REVIEW round 2: a SIGABRT handler that calls abort() (or any of the
fflush / raise / signal / rt_sigprocmask helpers transitively triggers
another abort) would re-enter the full sequence on the same stack and
recurse until stack overflow. Glibc's abort.c keeps an atomic depth
counter to short-circuit re-entry; mirror that.

On re-entrant invocation, skip flush/unblock/raise (they're what got
us back here) and go straight to sys_exit_group(134). This trades a
core dump on the inner abort for a clean exit instead of a stack
overflow crash that may not produce any usable diagnostic output.

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
